evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implementing data validation and transformation logic
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Responsibilities emphasise data validation, model maintenance, and troubleshooting performance with no velocity/speed language, making this rigour-dominated.
evidence.domain_risk: backend data infrastructure for global purchasing analytics
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role supports global purchasing analytics affecting business decisions at an automotive OEM without explicit regulatory or financial-reporting language, keeping it moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: An 8+ years experience bar, global purchasing scope, and a globally distributed team spanning EST overlap requirements signal an established, specialised organisation at scale.
evidence.jd_authorship: Develop, optimize, and maintain SQL / PySpark-based data models to support analytical applications
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise tools (Snowflake, Palantir Foundry, Databricks, PySpark, TypeScript) and specific technical tasks give a concrete Tuesday-morning picture, consistent with hiring-manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Partnering with analysts to translate requirements into backend solutions
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is the data/analytics function itself — analysts consuming backend data models — an internal_data orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Partnering with analysts to translate requirements into backend solutions
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role receives requirements from analysts and delivers scoped backend solutions rather than setting overall data strategy, an execution framing.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds backend data models and infrastructure that AI-driven tooling adoption downstream depends on, making them an ai_enabler.
evidence.testing_framing: Ability to troubleshoot performance issues in large datasets
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data validation appears as a named responsibility but without a distinct testing/data-quality framework or explicit ownership verb beyond implementation, closer to tool_listed than full responsibility framing.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: No compliance, audit, or regulatory risk language appears anywhere in the JD.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Develop, optimize, and maintain SQL / PySpark-based data models
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The dominant verbs are optimize/maintain/troubleshoot on existing infrastructure rather than building new systems from scratch, a fix_scale framing.
